---
title: "Tests for variable importance estimation"
author: "Stacy Cyrille"
date: last-modified
format: 
  html:
    keep-md: false
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
execute: 
  # echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
fig-width: 8
fig-align: "center"
fig-cap-location: top
html-math-method: mathml
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(MASS)
library(knitr)
library(survival)
library(timereg)
library(survML)
library(SuperLearner)
library(randomForestSRC)
library(survex)

rprojroot <- rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file()
source(file.path(rprojroot, "R/utils.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot, "R/generate_data.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot, "R/pred_generators.R"))
```

We generate data using additive hazards. 

```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(2026)

# Sample size 
n <- 1000

# Generate covariates
p <- 3
Sigma <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0,
                  0, 1, 0,
                  0, 0, 1), 
                3, 3)
X <- simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu = 0, Sigma)

# Simulate time to event data  
beta <- c(0, 0.02, 0.04)  
lambda0 <- 0.07
cens_rate <- 0.05
lambda_i <- lambda0 + drop(as.matrix(X) %*% beta)
lambda_i <- pmax(lambda_i, 1e-8)
T <- rexp(n, rate = lambda_i)
C <- rexp(n, rate = cens_rate)
time <- pmin(T, C)
event <- as.integer(T <= C)
data <- data.frame(time, event, X)
```


## Exclusion variable importance

We generate cross-fitting and sample-splitting folds.

```{r}
folds <- survML::generate_folds(n, V = 5, sample_split = TRUE)
table(folds$cf_folds)
table(folds$ss_folds)
```

We define time horizons, maximum follow-up time and times at which to approximate integrals.

```{r}
landmark_times <- quantile(time, c(0.25, 0.75))
restriction_time <- max(time[event == 1])
approx_times <- sort(unique(c(stats::quantile(time[event == 1 & time <= max(landmark_times)],
                                                    probs = seq(0, 1, by = 0.01)),
                                    landmark_times)))
length(approx_times)
```

### Aalen model 

We estimate the conditional survival functions, $F_0(t|X)$ and $G_0(t|X)$, using an additive Aalen model.

```{r}
conditional_surv_preds <- do.call(crossfit_surv_preds,
                                  c(list(time = time,
                                         event = event,
                                         X = X,
                                         folds = folds$cf_folds,
                                         newtimes = approx_times, 
                                         pred_generator = conditional_surv_aalen)))
names(conditional_surv_preds)
dim(conditional_surv_preds$S_hat[[1]])
dim(conditional_surv_preds$S_hat[[2]])
dim(conditional_surv_preds$S_hat_train[[1]])
```

Then, we estimate the full oracle prediction function, $f_{0}$, using Super Learner. 

```{r}
SL.library <- c("SL.mean","SL.glm")
V <- 2
large_oracle_generator_control <- list(indx = integer(0),
                                       outcome = "survival_probability",
                                       nuisance_preds = conditional_surv_preds,
                                       landmark_times = landmark_times,
                                       approx_times = approx_times, 
                                       restriction_time = restriction_time,
                                       SL.library = SL.library,
                                       V = V)
large_oracle_preds <- do.call(crossfit_oracle_preds,
                                  c(list(time = time,
                                         event = event,
                                         X = X,
                                         folds = folds$cf_folds,
                                         pred_generator = generate_oracle_predictions_DR),
                                    large_oracle_generator_control))
names(large_oracle_preds)
dim(large_oracle_preds$f_hat[[1]])
dim(large_oracle_preds$f_hat[[2]])
dim(large_oracle_preds$f_hat_train[[1]])
```

We finally compute variable importance estimates using Super Learner to estimate the residual oracle prediction $f_{0,s}$. 

```{r}
survML_aalen_auc <- list()
for (i in 1:ncol(X)){
  survML_aalen_auc[[i]] <-  vim(type = "AUC",
                                time = time,
                                event = event,
                                X = X,
                                landmark_times = landmark_times,
                                approx_times = approx_times, 
                                large_feature_vector = 1:ncol(X),
                                small_feature_vector = 1:ncol(X) %>% subset(.!=i),
                                conditional_surv_preds = conditional_surv_preds,
                                large_oracle_preds = large_oracle_preds,
                                small_oracle_generator_control = list(SL.library = SL.library,
                                                                      V = V),
                                cf_folds = folds$cf_folds,
                                ss_folds = folds$ss_folds, 
                                scale_est = TRUE)
}
survML_aalen_auc %>%
  map(~.x$result)%>%
  bind_rows()%>%
  kable()
```

### Global survival stacking  

We estimate the conditional survival functions, $F_0(t|X)$ and $G_0(t|X)$, using global survival stacking.

```{r}
bin_size <- 0.5
conditional_surv_generator_control <- list(SL.library = SL.library,
                                           V = V,
                                           bin_size = bin_size,
                                           approx_times = approx_times)
conditional_surv_preds <- do.call(crossfit_surv_preds,
                                  c(list(time = time,
                                         event = event,
                                         X = X,
                                         folds = folds$cf_folds,
                                         newtimes = approx_times, 
                                         pred_generator = generate_nuisance_predictions_stackG), 
                                    conditional_surv_generator_control))
names(conditional_surv_preds)
dim(conditional_surv_preds$S_hat[[1]])
dim(conditional_surv_preds$S_hat[[2]])
dim(conditional_surv_preds$S_hat_train[[1]])
```

Then, we estimate the full oracle prediction function, $f_{0}$, using Super Learner. 

```{r}
large_oracle_generator_control <- list(indx = integer(0),
                                       outcome = "survival_probability",
                                       nuisance_preds = conditional_surv_preds,
                                       landmark_times = landmark_times,
                                       approx_times = approx_times, 
                                       restriction_time = restriction_time,
                                       SL.library = SL.library,
                                       V = V)
large_oracle_preds <- do.call(crossfit_oracle_preds,
                              c(list(time = time,
                                     event = event,
                                     X = X,
                                     folds = folds$cf_folds,
                                     pred_generator = generate_oracle_predictions_DR),
                                large_oracle_generator_control))
names(large_oracle_preds)
dim(large_oracle_preds$f_hat[[1]])
dim(large_oracle_preds$f_hat[[2]])
dim(large_oracle_preds$f_hat_train[[1]])
```

We finally compute variable importance estimates using Super Learner to estimate the residual oracle prediction $f_{0,s}$. 

```{r}
survML_stackG_auc <- list()
for (i in 1:ncol(X)){
  survML_stackG_auc[[i]] <- vim(type = "AUC",
                                time = time,
                                event = event,
                                X = X,
                                landmark_times = landmark_times,
                                approx_times = approx_times, 
                                large_feature_vector = 1:ncol(X),
                                small_feature_vector = 1:ncol(X) %>% subset(.!=i),
                                conditional_surv_preds = conditional_surv_preds,
                                large_oracle_preds = large_oracle_preds,
                                small_oracle_generator_control = list(SL.library = SL.library,
                                                                      V = V),
                                cf_folds = folds$cf_folds,
                                ss_folds = folds$ss_folds, 
                                scale_est = TRUE)
}
survML_stackG_auc %>%
  map(~.x$result)%>%
  bind_rows()%>%
  kable()
```

## Permutation variable importance 

### Aalen model

```{r}
# Fit the Aalen model
aalen_fit <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~const(R1)+R2+R3, data = data,
                            resample.iid = 1)

# Model performance 
aalen_exp <- survex::explain(aalen_fit, data = data[,c("R1","R2","R3")],
                             y = Surv(data$time, data$event), label = "aalen")
aalen_perf <- model_performance(aalen_exp, metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc, `Brier score` = brier_score))
plot(aalen_perf)

times <- quantile(data$time, c(0.25, 0.75))
aalen_perf_times <- model_performance(aalen_exp, metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc, `Brier score` = brier_score), times = times)
aalen_perf_times$result

# Variable importance
permut_aalen_brier <- model_parts(
  aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference",
  loss_function = survex::loss_brier_score
  )
plot(permut_aalen_brier)
# model_parts(
#   aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference",
#   loss_function = survex::loss_brier_score,
#   times = times
#   )
permut_aalen_auc <- model_parts(
  aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference",
  loss_function = survex::loss_one_minus_cd_auc
  )
plot(permut_aalen_auc)
# model_parts(
#   aalen_exp, B = 10, type = "difference",
#   loss_function = survex::loss_one_minus_cd_auc,
#   times = times 
#   )
```

### Random survival forests

```{r}
# Fit random survival forests
rfsrc_fit <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Surv(time, event)~., data, 
                                    importance = "none",
                                    splitrule = "logrank",
                                    seed = 2024)

# Model performance 
rfsrc_exp <- survex::explain(rfsrc_fit, data = data[,c("R1","R2","R3")],
                             y = Surv(data$time, data$event))
rfsrc_perf <- model_performance(rfsrc_exp, metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc, `Brier score` = brier_score))
plot(rfsrc_perf)

times <- quantile(data$time, c(0.25, 0.75))
rfsrc_perf_times <- model_performance(rfsrc_exp, metrics = c(`C/D AUC` = cd_auc, `Brier score` = brier_score), times = times)
rfsrc_perf_times$result

# Variable importance 
permut_rfsrc_brier <- model_parts(rfsrc_exp, B = 10, type = "difference",
                                  loss_function = survex::loss_brier_score)
plot(permut_rfsrc_brier) 
permut_rfsrc_auc <- model_parts(rfsrc_exp, B = 10, type = "difference",
                                loss_function = survex::loss_one_minus_cd_auc)
plot(permut_rfsrc_auc)
```
